title: Void elements
description: Tags that don't take a closing tag.

questions.prompt: Which is a void element (no closing tag)?

In HTML5, self-closing slashes on void elements (
) are:
options: Allowed but optional — a style choice
questions.explanation:
HTML5 parsers accept
and
. Pick one style and stick with it.
<hr> is a horizontal rule — void element.

questions.prompt: Which is NOT a void element?
questions.explanation: <script> can have content (inline JS) — needs a closing tag.

questions.prompt: How many attributes can a void element have?
questions.explanation: Void just means 'no content'. Attribute count is unrestricted.

questions.prompt: Which void element is for line breaks?

options: No — both are accepted, self-closing / is optional
questions.explanation: Both parse the same in HTML5. Pick a style and stick with it.

questions.prompt: Which is used to embed a CSS file (void element)?
questions.explanation: <link> is void — src via href attribute.
